item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it! Granted, it could use a bit of proofreading for syntax sake, but the concept is interesting and the overall execution is hilarious!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wonder what would happen if the object was removed during the debate but not by the debaters? Perhaps an automated timed device. Would the debate end? Would they all freak out over it?

Also, I am amused by the idea of the world ending if this breached containment and everyone starving to death because they become too busy arguing over something trivial, like whether a particular block of wood would be a better doorstop or a paperweight.

+1 despite the inconsistency that looks to be here to stay ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
hmmm…
Came here to point out what I thought was a minor inconsistency, only to find out it's an unresolved point that has been debated more or less nonstop since first posted.

Wouldn't it be marvelous if ModernMajorGeneral did this on purpose? A skip about debate that directly spawned an interminable debate of the exact type described in the skip. Like a subtler version of Samothrace.

Don't care if actually true, this is now my meta-headcanon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"If this protocol in conjunction with standard quarantine measures fails to contain the emergency, personnel should refer to Plan MODERATE-4, which [DATA EXPUNGED]"… NEVER redact the Containment Procedures. What are personnel supposed to do in case of an emergeny? Eat Ketercakes? Just give up and shoot their own heads? Activate the on-site nuclear warhead? Procedure 110-Montauk?
The last test also has a little bit of LOLFoundation…
-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I came here to say the same thing five months later. This really hasn't aged well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and the writer doesn't care to do anything about it

If they don't want to change it, that's their perogative.
People can provide all the critique they want, but it's up to the author whether they want to take it or not. Considering the article is sitting at +153 as I write this, it's clearly not that big a deal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: -1 Downvoted for not addressing the controversy around Test 1101-1E in five years. Obviously people see a flaw with it, some even offering good advice, and the writer doesn't care to do anything about it.

item.title: Umm...
item.content:encoded: So, in the post, it says, "no physical fighting" but later in the test log it says there were casualties?

item.title: Yet another complaint about the contradiction
item.content:encoded: Wonderful idea but 1101-1E just kills the momentum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why would the containment room be 20x20? That is DAMN BIG. Also, dropping Nerve Gas seems unreasonable to contain affected individuals, why not put them to sleep & contain them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote…. Cuz if no conclusion is reached it just ends. That's not an anomaly that's an argument.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I understand why people are saying that it's inconsistent, but it's actually possible for casualties to occur for reasons other than interpersonal violence. The [DATA EXPUNGED] is basically saying "use your imagination" — not particularly elegant, no, but it's not inconsistent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Khym Chanur noticed it first, but I just kept thinking about somebody hopping onto the pedestal. It seems like the UNWISE foundation equivalent to sitting on the photocopier at the holiday party. I can imagine some very dumb jr. researcher going in with a few attractive persons of their preferred gender and hopping on. The amusing thing is that (even apart from possible disciplinary reassignment to D-class) the results might not be what they'd hoped.

"In conclusion, G████ is, as evidenced by this stunt, the worst researcher, and possibly one of the most valueless people, I have ever met." [walks forward, grabs G████ by the ear, pulling him off the pedestal.] "Come on, 'prize,' I've got some plans for you. How'd you like to try [redacted] with SCP-████?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the article a lot, but I must agree with the other users on that inconsistency. You wrote that no one displayed violence, but casualties were reported after that political part. Also, what would happen if you placed more than one object on the pedestal?

No vote until inconsistency is fixed up.

item.title: Image Usage
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1101's image was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact Dr. Poke or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

E: Permission for image usage has been granted, as long as the proper credit is given to Antique Helper Auctions. As this is the author's decision, I will not reinstate the image, but if credit is given, he may do so himself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting concept, but I felt like the use of "[REDACTED]" was… well, overused. It honestly seemed like the censoring was just there because it could be, if you understand what I mean. I believe the very last use of "[REDACTED]" is worth mentioning; if we don't know how many people it would take to start an argument-pocalypse, then how are we supposed to safely prevent it? Sure, safety measures could be taken to prevent arguments but shouldn't people know the dangerous number of arguing people so the facility could be evacuated if that number is reached?
+1 for the idea, but I really think the use of "[REDACTED]" should be lowered in this article, if not removed completely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm guessing not…I was wondering the same thing.

Though I must also point out, violence is not required in order for there to be deaths - there are many things that can kill a human being. If, say, the fact that a topic is already extremely controversial before put on the pedestal makes people have strokes or heart attacks from severe stress when it is on the pedestal, that might explain it - someone shouts an opinion so emphatically that it makes them burst a blood vessel, people hear it and get equally worked up over the topic and start shouting things so hard they kill themselves, other people happen to hear the shouts, and it all just spreads…There's even a place for headsplosion, if you wanted to get really crazy.

Of course, in that case, the [DATA EXPUNGED] is what's bad about that log entry, because it makes readers think people started killing each other when the real answer is both more interesting and more consistent with the description.

By the way, love the scip, upvoted. XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the author still around as the whole contradiction issue has been brought up fairly often and yet no response has been made about it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this now. I don't know why I had it downvoted, but that has been rectified.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Guilliman is right, the report is inconsistent.

In Description, Paragraph Three, the last sentence reads:

Interestingly, no incidents of physical violence have been recorded during an event despite frequent displays of extreme anger.

Experiment 1101-E then says:

Result: [DATA EXPUNGED] and █ casualties before containment was reestablished. Security measures for SCP-1011's use with controversial items have been heightened.

One of these things is not like the other. One of these clauses has to be omitted or altered in order to make this SCiP self-consistent. Holding my vote pending an edit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved the test logs. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As the (re)writer of 946 I think it's sufficiently different in functionality that really there's no overlap. The fact that they both deal with debates doesn't really make them that similar beyond a superficial level.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm curious what would happen if a subject was determined to be entirely agreed upon in as numerous ways as possible between two people, and had that subject put on the pedestal and those two subjects put in proximity of the pedestal. Would their opinions of the subject change?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It says there is no violence but the political one said there were casualties

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens if someone puts 050 on it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know that we're supposed to avoid cross-testing SCPs, but I kinda wish we could put this in the same room as scp-946 just to see if anything happens.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
XK-class end of the world is the Republicans/Democrats winning the next presidential election

Wrong.

CK-class restructuring. It's change we can believe in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Such discussion is better served in the thread for such discussions. Thank you. -Bright

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This doesn't jive. I suggest the retarded political joke entry be removed. It breaks tone and is explicitly against the way the object functions as per the description.

I still upvoted, but yoric's right. Kill or fix (preferably kill) that addition.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually - history lesson! - XK started out as religious apocalypse, and turned into 'salted earth'. And CK is generally thought to mean reality restructuring (though, again, no canon). Plus, and this is just me personally, but I always assumed that RK referred to any non-standard life forms taking over an ecosystem, not just robots… "just robots" seems awfully specific.

There's also GH ("dead greenhouse"; planet is fine, life as we know it mostly wiped out), SK dominance shift (new Earth dominant species!), and if you go into Tales, BI biological invasion, IK natural disaster, ZK reality failure. Plus a few more that have never really been defined (BK, EK, XC).

Of course, if an article decides a CK-class restructuring is an invasion of psychedelic locusts and an XK-class end of the world is the Republicans/Democrats winning the next presidential election, there's nothing wrong with that (besides the lameness of the examples, of course :P). No canon and all of that. I just happen to enjoy this topic.

PS though it would be pretty hilarious to start an internet debate about the subject considering the nature of the SCP :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First off: There is no canon and you can make up whatever definitions you want for whatever scenarios you want.

Second: I love how you chose to use an "AK-class scenario". Last year I went on an archive binge and looked up all the K-class scenarios and thought that "everyone goes permanently crazy" was missing. I literally wrote down a note reading:

If minds are transformed to the point of going mad, it's an AK-class end of the world scenario (or not?)

So, yeah - color me gleeful to see that someone else thought of something similar. :D

Third: If you're interested in consistency (which, let me reiterate, is not a requirement), you're still fine. An "AK-class scenario" has been referenced in the short story Change as a scenario that implies a shift in reality. I think your version fits with that just fine (even though it didn't have to!); CK-class typically is thought of as involving restructuring reality (rewriting history, change in the way the universe works, etc), while AK-class could refer to a restructuring of subjective reality in such a way as to destroy anything we (as in, humans as we are now) could recognize or accept as reality. End of the world as we know it via madness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seriously, someone has to explain what there is to like about this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, hang on.

Interestingly, no incidents of physical violence have been recorded during an event despite frequent displays of extreme anger.

1101-1E
Object: One political campaign button reading '[REDACTED] 2012'
Test Subjects: 5 D-class personnel D-1011-1 through 5
Additional Instructions: None
Transcript:
[DATA EXPUNGED]
Result: [DATA EXPUNGED] and █ casualties before containment was reestablished. Security measures for SCP-1011's use with controversial items have been heightened.

This doesn't jive. I suggest the retarded political joke entry be removed. It breaks tone and is explicitly against the way the object functions as per the description.

I really like this article, but I'm downvoting until this is corrected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: K-class situations typically refer to "end of blah blah blah" scenarios with no consensus on what means what. Personally, I've seen XK refer to end of universe type scenarios, CK refer to "grey goo" type scenarios, and something else refer to the loss of civilization scenarios.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And… what? Seriously, it doesn't seem to be anything of interest. It makes you have arguments until someone gives up?

Maybe if he ever does the log file that's linked to it, but I doubt it.

item.title: Public Forum
item.content:encoded:
This makes me think of Greek orators of antiquity in public forums.

This is one of my favorite articles now.

I just recommend you get rid of that dead link.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Question, the description states no actual violence has been commited by participants in a 1101 event. Yet you included an expunged-test-log-with-huge-casualties, and it doesn't appear to be the several-months-until-they-starved test. How come?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, if somebody sat on the thing, what would the observers argue about? And what would the winner do with the person sitting on it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As much as I like this, I cannot in good conscience upvote an entry that includes a link to a nonexistent page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not usually a fan of heavily redacted entries, but I really liked the experiment log about the campaign button. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nah, that's probably an OK, as in okaaaaay. Whatevs.

I'm pretty sure that XK is the only hard-and-fast apocalypse scenario, the others can be whatever you want them to be. I've also seen YK and ZK, the latter of which has referred to reality itself stop giving a shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A subtly scary object. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, it's totally fine to make up new K-class scenarios. I think they're really cool, and give you an insight as to how the Foundation thinks. So far, there's XK, which is usually agreed to mean religous type end of the world scenarios, RK, when robots out-perform and overtake the Earth's ecosystem, NK, when something self-replicates to the point where the planet becomes uninhabitable, and CK, when society is overhauled into an unrecognizable form. For AK, I assume the A stands for apathy, where the human race collectively stops giving a shit and dies of starvation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice concept, cute test logs, a good change of pace from the "It kills everyone related to it" SCPs. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It could be similar because of the debate or causing conflict but I believe it stands for itself. Would be nice to have a experiment log like SCP-914, SCP-447 etc. Good job upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: From what I read in the logs, it looks like the answer for your second question would be that they might agree on the object itself, but would then debate (or, likely, argue petulantly) about things RELATED to the object. Such as "This is a stunning example of cave crystal." "Oh yes. Such a shame we have to destroy their natural environment to appreciate them." "Well not so much, it WILL grow back after all." "Yes in fifteen thousand years! All just to have a fancy centerpiece?" "Well if mankind wasn't MEANT to have the control over nature that we do, we would have been wiped out by now!" Etc. etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Two questions: 1) If two people who are used to debating each other use the SCP, does it just look like another of their debates? 2) If both people agree on the discussed topic, does one just play Devil's Advocate? And then does his opinion remain that way even after that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure how to improve the execution - there could be more content on either results of the 'contest' or a recovery story but I'm not sure if either of those would be an improvement. There is always the not-started experiment log to do, I originally conceived this as a log-based sort of SCP.

I don't think SCP-114 is particularly similar but yeah SCP-946 is. I didn't realise I gave them almost the same name (no this is not actually named internet forums). I think I get away with the similarities considering that 'discussion' is not a very common SCP topic, if this were a 'scary door' this similar to another scary door I would think differently.

As to the XK classification, I was under the impression there were a bunch of classifications and XK is sort of a 'everything blows up' event, which this isn't so I made one up. If it's too silly I'll change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. However, if it's really supposed to be Internet forums, there should never be a winner and every conversation should end with the mutual decision to laugh out loud and begin the undertaking of presenting and critiquing various pictures of breasts.

More seriously: Should this be a -J? Also, have you read SCP-946 or SCP-114, which are somewhat similar? Finally, is "AK-class" the term you want? XK-class is usually used to denote an end of the world scenario.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea, but the exicution is a bit lacking maybe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1101, 'Internet Forums'.
